proven.claim: At line 338 the path condition fn?.type === "member_expression" holds.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; At line 338 the path condition fn?.type === "member_expression" holds.
; Prove: fn cannot be null at this point.
; Optional chaining: null?.type === undefined ≠ "member_expression",
; so path_cond_holds = 1 forces fn_null = 0.

(declare-const fn_null         Int)   ; 1 = fn is null,  0 = fn is non-null
(declare-const path_cond_holds Int)   ; 1 = fn?.type === "member_expression" is true

; Code semantics of optional chaining:
; fn is null → fn?.type = undefined → condition is false
(assert (=> (= fn_null 1) (= path_cond_holds 0)))

; Known fact: path condition is satisfied (given in problem context)
(assert (= path_cond_holds 1))

; Negation of property: fn IS null when path condition holds
(assert (= fn_null 1))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — fn cannot be null at line 338

proven.claim: Prove: when prop is non-null and prop.text ∈ {toString, toFixed, toUpperCase},
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; Prove: when prop is non-null and prop.text ∈ {toString, toFixed, toUpperCase},
; inferType provably returns "string" (not null, not "number").
; Return encoding: 0 = null, 1 = "number", 2 = "string"

(declare-const prop_is_null     Int)  ; 0 = non-null
(declare-const prop_text_match  Int)  ; 1 = prop.text is toString/toFixed/toUpperCase
(declare-const inferred         Int)  ; return value of inferType

; Code transition (line 339):
;   if (prop?.text === "toString" || prop?.text === "toFixed" ||
;       prop?.text === "toUpperCase") return "string";
; Encoded as: prop non-null AND text matches → return "string"
(assert (=> (and (= prop_is_null 0) (= prop_text_match 1))
            (= inferred 2)))

; Establish preconditions
(assert (= prop_is_null 0))
(assert (= prop_text_match 1))

; Negate the property: inferred ≠ "string"
(assert (not (= inferred 2)))

(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — the string-method branch provably returns "string"
